title: Lemma 2.3: prove the sign identity and cross-term cancellation in the projection length formula

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The 'completing the square' proof of the projection length formula
|x_C|^2 = (1/tau) sum_{A,B} F_{A,B}(x) hinges on the identity

  tau|x|^2 = sum_{A,B} F_{A,B}(x) + sum_R C_R(x)

obtained via three bijections. The crux, ASSERTED but not derived in problem.tex, is the sign relation for a unicyclic R with two cycle edges e,e':

  epsilon_{A,B,e} * epsilon_{A,B,e'} = - delta_{R,e} * delta_{R,e'},

where {A,B} is the 2-forest obtained by deleting e,e' from R. A complete proof must:
(a) establish this sign identity from the definitions of chi_{A,B} (cut vector) and omega_R (circulation), using that deleting e,e' from the cycle C_R splits G into exactly the two trees A,B and that the two edges are oppositely oriented relative to the cut vs. the cycle;
(b) expand F (eq:flux_formula) and C (eq:circulation_formula) and show the diagonal terms sum to tau|x|^2 and the off-diagonal cross terms cancel exactly;
(c) conclude by killing the circulation terms via x_C orthogonal to Flow(G).

Numerically verified on triangle, a multigraph, and K4. Difficulty: medium-high (sign bookkeeping under the orientation conventions).
